articles.headline: Tragedy Strikes Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela: At Least 30 Dead, Dozens Injured in Crowd Crush
articles.explainer:
• A devastating crowd crush occurred at the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, claiming at least 30 lives and injuring over 60.
• The incident reportedly took place near a major bathing ghat during a significant ritual, leading to chaos among pilgrims.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the causes, focusing on crowd management protocols, entry/exit points, and emergency response mechanisms.
• This incident highlights critical challenges in managing large religious gatherings, emphasizing the need for advanced planning, technology deployment, and inter-agency coordination.
• The Prime Minister and Chief Minister have expressed condolences and announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: The 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is being held in Prayagraj (formerly Allahabad).
Kumbh Mela is one of the largest peaceful gatherings in the world, held every 12 years at four different locations.
Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides for effective management of disasters.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic failures in crowd management at large religious gatherings in India and suggest comprehensive reforms.
Discuss the role of technology (e.g., AI, surveillance, data analytics) in preventing and responding to crowd crushes.
Evaluate the responsibilities of state and central governments in ensuring safety at events of national and international significance like the Kumbh Mela.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such tragedies on the local community and the broader religious tourism sector.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of a large gathering at the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, with emergency services responding to an incident.
articles.trivia: The Kumbh Mela was inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2017.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues

articles.headline: Parliament Passes National Education Technology Board Bill 2025, Aims to Integrate AI in Learning
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Parliament has successfully passed the National Education Technology Board (NETB) Bill, 2025, marking a significant step towards modernizing the education sector.
• The Bill proposes the establishment of a statutory body, the NETB, to facilitate the integration of technology, especially Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), into teaching and learning processes.
• Its mandate includes developing national standards for educational technology, recommending digital curricula, and overseeing the deployment of AI-powered learning tools.
• Critics raise concerns about digital divide, data privacy, and the potential impact on traditional teaching methods.
• Proponents highlight the potential for personalized learning, wider access to quality education, and preparing students for future job markets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Education
prelimsPoints: The NETB Bill 2025 aims to integrate technology in education.
The proposed NETB will be a statutory body.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 laid emphasis on the use of technology in education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of integrating AI and ML in India's education system, considering socio-economic disparities.
Discuss how the NETB can ensure equitable access to digital education tools and mitigate the digital divide.
Critically evaluate the provisions of the NETB Bill 2025 regarding data privacy and ethical AI use in educational settings.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in scaling up educational technology initiatives across the country.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image showing students interacting with AI-powered learning platforms.
articles.trivia: India's digital learning platform, SWAYAM, offers online courses for free to students across various disciplines.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Development processes and the development industry - the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% in its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting.
• This decision comes amidst persistent concerns over retail inflation, which continues to hover above the RBI's comfort zone, driven by food price volatility.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target range while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, monitoring global economic uncertainties and domestic demand conditions.
• The move is expected to maintain stability in the financial markets but may impact borrowing costs for businesses and consumers in the short term.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates.
Inflation targeting is a key objective of the RBI's monetary policy framework.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global and domestic economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing supply-side inflation.
Examine the implications of a prolonged high-interest rate regime on different sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the current state of India's inflation dynamics and the factors contributing to its persistence.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Focus on Services and IP
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their conclusive phase, with both nations pushing to finalize the deal.
• Key sticking points remain in areas such as market access for services, intellectual property rights, and rules of origin.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially doubling trade by 2030.
• India is seeking greater access for its professionals and textiles, while the UK is keen on reducing tariffs on Scotch whisky and automotive products.
• A successful agreement is anticipated to strengthen strategic ties and diversify supply chains for both countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and the UK are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
Services and Intellectual Property are key areas of negotiation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-UK FTA for India's foreign policy and economic objectives.
Discuss the potential impact of the FTA on key Indian sectors such as IT services, textiles, and agriculture.
Examine the challenges in negotiating comprehensive FTAs, particularly concerning sensitive sectors and intellectual property rights.
Evaluate how this FTA could reshape India's trade relations with other major economies post-Brexit.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: The UK is one of India's largest trading partners and a significant source of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, 'EOS-08', aboard the PSLV-C58 from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is equipped with high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and optical sensors, designed to provide enhanced data for agriculture, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The mission also carried several student-developed nano-satellites as co-passengers, promoting space technology education.
• This launch reinforces India's capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for national development.
• The satellite is expected to significantly improve India's ability to monitor natural resources and respond to environmental challenges.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
PSLV is one of ISRO's primary launch vehicles.
Earth Observation Satellites are used for monitoring Earth's resources and environment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-08 for India's sustainable development goals and disaster preparedness.
Analyze how indigenous space technology contributes to India's strategic autonomy and regional leadership.
Examine the role of student and academic participation in ISRO's missions and its impact on fostering scientific temper.
Evaluate the potential applications of high-resolution remote sensing data in sectors like agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from Sriharikota, with the ISRO logo visible.
articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries to have successfully launched a mission to Mars (Mangalyaan).

articles.headline: Government Unveils National AI Strategy 2.0, Focus on Ethical AI and Skilling
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government has launched its updated National AI Strategy 2.0, building upon the initial framework with a greater emphasis on ethical AI development and a robust skilling ecosystem.
• The strategy outlines plans for increased public and private investment in AI research and development, particularly in healthcare, agriculture, and smart cities.
• A key component is the establishment of a National AI Ethics Council to frame guidelines for responsible AI deployment and address concerns like bias and accountability.
• The policy also focuses on creating a skilled AI workforce through specialized training programs and academic collaborations.
• This initiative aims to position India as a global leader in AI innovation while ensuring equitable and inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India's National AI Strategy 2.0 focuses on ethical AI and skilling.
The strategy aims to establish a National AI Ethics Council.
AI applications are being promoted in sectors like healthcare and agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical components of India's National AI Strategy 2.0 and its potential to transform various sectors of the economy.
Analyze the ethical dilemmas and regulatory challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI, and how the new strategy addresses them.
Examine the importance of a robust skilling ecosystem in realizing India's AI ambitions and mitigating job displacement concerns.
Critically evaluate India's position in the global AI landscape and the steps needed to achieve leadership in ethical AI development.
articles.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected AI circuits and data streams, symbolizing innovation.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and AI talent availability.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Strictly Enforce Pollution Norms in NCR, Cites Health Emergency
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has issued stringent directives to the states comprising the National Capital Region (NCR) to ensure strict enforcement of air pollution control norms.
• Citing the ongoing public health emergency caused by severe air quality, the court emphasized zero tolerance for violations related to industrial emissions, construction dust, and vehicular pollution.
• The directive includes the formation of special task forces, real-time monitoring of pollution sources, and immediate penalties for non-compliance.
• This intervention underscores the judiciary's increasing role in environmental governance and public health protection.
• States are mandated to submit action taken reports within a month, detailing measures implemented and their effectiveness.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court is involved in environmental governance, especially concerning air pollution in the NCR.
The Air Quality Index (AQI) measures air pollution levels.
The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, is a key legislation for environmental protection in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the persistent challenges in controlling air pollution in the NCR despite judicial interventions and governmental policies.
Discuss the effectiveness of judicial activism in environmental protection, particularly in balancing development and ecological concerns.
Examine the socio-economic implications of severe air pollution on public health, productivity, and tourism in the NCR.
Suggest comprehensive multi-sectoral strategies, including technological solutions and behavioral changes, to achieve sustainable air quality.
articles.imageDescription: A hazy skyline of Delhi, depicting severe air pollution.
articles.trivia: The 'odd-even' road rationing scheme was first introduced in Delhi in 2016 to combat air pollution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Air Pollution, Causes, Effects and Control Measures

articles.headline: Coastal Security Exercise 'Sagar Kavach' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• A two-day extensive coastal security exercise, 'Sagar Kavach', successfully concluded along India's coastline, involving multiple agencies.
• The exercise simulated various maritime threats, including infiltration attempts, terrorist attacks from the sea, and illegal fishing, to test the preparedness and coordination of coastal security forces.
• Participants included the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, Marine Police, Customs, and other relevant state and central agencies.
• The drill aimed to plug gaps in coastal defense mechanisms identified post the 26/11 Mumbai attacks and enhance real-time information sharing.
• The exercise highlighted the need for continuous training, technological upgrades, and seamless integration among all stakeholders for robust maritime security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: 'Sagar Kavach' is a coastal security exercise.
It involves agencies like the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, and Marine Police.
The exercise aims to enhance India's maritime preparedness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of maritime security threats to India and the strategies employed to counter them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-agency coordination mechanisms in India's coastal security framework, citing examples like 'Sagar Kavach'.
Examine the role of technology and intelligence sharing in strengthening India's maritime domain awareness.
Suggest measures to further enhance the capabilities of coastal security forces, including infrastructure development and capacity building.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard ships patrolling the coastline during a security exercise.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, making coastal security a critical concern.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Mission Poshan 2.0' to Combat Malnutrition with Integrated Approach
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched 'Mission Poshan 2.0', an integrated nutrition support program aimed at tackling malnutrition across India.
• The mission consolidates existing nutrition-related schemes and introduces new components focusing on dietary diversity, traditional food systems, and community-based management of severe acute malnutrition.
• It emphasizes the use of technology for real-time monitoring, data analytics, and last-mile delivery of services through Anganwadi centers.
• The program seeks to address both undernutrition and micronutrient deficiencies among children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
• 'Mission Poshan 2.0' aligns with India's commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: 'Mission Poshan 2.0' is a nutrition support program.
It targets children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Anganwadi centers play a crucial role in its implementation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of malnutrition in India and how 'Mission Poshan 2.0' attempts to address them through an integrated approach.
Discuss the role of technology, community participation, and inter-sectoral convergence in the effective implementation of nutrition programs.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving nutrition security in India, particularly in remote and vulnerable communities.
Examine how 'Mission Poshan 2.0' aligns with global targets like SDG 2 and India's progress towards these goals.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a balanced diet and healthy children, symbolizing the goals of Mission Poshan.
articles.trivia: The Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Policy Framework for Green Hydrogen Production and Export
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of New and Renewable Energy has unveiled a comprehensive policy framework aimed at boosting the production and export of Green Hydrogen.
• The policy offers incentives for establishing green hydrogen and ammonia production facilities, including waivers on inter-state transmission charges for renewable energy used in production.
• It emphasizes developing dedicated manufacturing zones and promoting R&D in electrolysis technology to reduce production costs.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen, reduce reliance on fossil fuels, and achieve energy security goals.
• Environmental groups welcome the move, but stress the need for robust regulatory oversight and sustainable water management practices.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Green Hydrogen is produced using renewable energy.
India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched to facilitate this.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Green Hydrogen as a key driver for India's energy transition and its role in achieving net-zero emission targets.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of promoting green hydrogen production and export, along with associated challenges.
Examine the policy incentives and infrastructure development required to scale up green hydrogen production in India.
Evaluate the global implications of India becoming a major green hydrogen exporter and its impact on international energy markets.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but pure hydrogen (H2) is rare on Earth and must be produced.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Site in Odisha, Reveals Rich Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastery site in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has revealed well-preserved stupas, monastic cells, statues of Buddha, and intricate terracotta panels dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• This finding adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, which is already known for sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri.
• The discovery is expected to provide new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural styles.
• Conservation efforts are underway, and plans for developing the site for tourism and academic research are being considered.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage (e.g., Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri).
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the evolution and spread of Buddhism in India.
Examine the challenges and best practices in conserving ancient archaeological sites while promoting tourism and research.
Analyze the contribution of Odisha's historical sites to India's cultural tapestry and their potential for socio-economic development.
Evaluate the role of ASI in protecting and promoting India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient site, revealing parts of a stupa and ancient pottery.
articles.trivia: The first Director-General of the Archaeological Survey of India was Alexander Cunningham in 1871.

articles.headline: Report Highlights India's Demographic Dividend Amidst Rapid Urbanization Challenges
articles.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog underscores India's unique position to harness its demographic dividend over the next two decades, with a large working-age population.
• However, the report also warns about the escalating challenges posed by rapid and often unplanned urbanization, including strain on infrastructure, housing, and public services.
• It recommends strategic investments in education, skill development, and job creation in both urban and semi-urban areas to fully capitalize on the youth bulge.
• The report also calls for innovative urban planning, smart city initiatives, and sustainable infrastructure development to accommodate population shifts.
• Failure to address these challenges could transform the demographic dividend into a demographic disaster.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Effects of globalization on Indian society
prelimsPoints: Demographic dividend refers to the economic growth potential that can result from shifts in a population’s age structure.
NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
Urbanization presents challenges like infrastructure strain and housing shortages.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges presented by India's demographic dividend in the context of rapid urbanization.
Discuss the policy interventions required in education, skill development, and employment generation to leverage the youth population effectively.
Examine the strategies for sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development to manage the pressures of increasing urban populations.
Critically evaluate the 'demographic disaster' scenario and the preventive measures India must undertake.
articles.imageDescription: A composite image showing a bustling city skyline alongside young, educated individuals, representing urbanization and demographic dividend.
articles.trivia: India is projected to become the world's most populous country by 2027, surpassing China.

articles.headline: Indian Naval Ship Participates in Multinational Maritime Exercise 'MILAN 2025'
articles.explainer:
• An Indian Naval Ship (INS) has joined several other nations in the multinational maritime exercise 'MILAN 2025' in the Andaman Sea.
• 'MILAN' is a biennial multilateral naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy, aiming to enhance interoperability and foster naval cooperation among friendly navies.
• This year's exercise focuses on anti-submarine warfare, anti-piracy operations, and humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) scenarios.
• The participation underscores India's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening maritime security partnerships.
• The exercise provides a platform for participating navies to share best practices and build confidence in joint operations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: 'MILAN' is a biennial multilateral naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy.
It takes place in the Andaman Sea.
HADR operations are a key focus of such exercises.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of multilateral naval exercises like 'MILAN' for India's maritime security and foreign policy objectives in the Indo-Pacific.
Analyze how such exercises contribute to building interoperability and trust among regional navies, especially in HADR scenarios.
Examine the evolving security landscape in the Indo-Pacific and India's role in maintaining a free, open, and inclusive region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of defense diplomacy and military cooperation in strengthening bilateral and multilateral relations.
articles.imageDescription: Warships from various nations sailing in formation during a naval exercise.
articles.trivia: The MILAN exercise was first initiated in 1995 with the participation of four littoral states.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends GST Rationalization and Inclusion of Petroleum Products
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee on finance has submitted a report recommending a rationalization of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) structure.
• The panel advocated for fewer tax slabs and greater clarity in classification to simplify the indirect tax regime for businesses and consumers.
• Crucially, the report strongly urged the government to consider bringing petroleum products, electricity, and alcohol under the ambit of GST.
• This move, if implemented, could lead to more uniform pricing across states and potentially higher revenue collection for the government.
• However, state governments have historically resisted including these items due to their significant revenue generation potential under existing state taxes.
gsSubTopics: Government Budgeting
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: GST is an indirect tax levied on the supply of goods and services.
The GST Council is the governing body for GST.
Petroleum products, electricity, and alcohol are currently outside GST's ambit.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against bringing petroleum products, electricity, and alcohol under GST, considering fiscal federalism and state revenues.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges of GST rationalization, including its impact on inflation and ease of doing business.
Examine the role of parliamentary committees in policy formulation and oversight of economic reforms.
Evaluate the journey of GST in India since its inception and the reforms still needed for its optimal functioning.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of the GST logo with various tax slabs being simplified.
articles.trivia: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, replacing multiple cascading taxes levied by the central and state governments.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Climate Finance Working Group Meeting, Pushes for Equitable Climate Action
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the first G20 Climate Finance Working Group (CFWG) meeting of the year, focusing on mobilizing funds for climate action in developing countries.
• The meeting aims to formulate strategies for increasing climate finance flows, enhancing accessibility, and addressing the debt burden faced by many nations.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate financing.
• Discussions include innovative financing mechanisms, adaptation funding, and technology transfer to support a just energy transition.
• The outcomes of this meeting are crucial for setting the agenda for climate negotiations at upcoming international forums.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: G20 is an international forum for the governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
CBDR-RC is a principle under the UNFCCC.
Climate finance aims to support mitigation and adaptation actions.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's position and priorities at the G20 Climate Finance Working Group meeting, particularly concerning equitable climate action.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing adequate and accessible climate finance for developing countries and the role of developed nations.
Examine the concept of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate innovative financing mechanisms and policy frameworks that can accelerate the just energy transition in developing economies.
articles.imageDescription: The G20 logo with flags of member countries, set against a backdrop of renewable energy infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The G20 accounts for about 85% of global GDP, 75% of global trade, and two-thirds of the world population.

articles.headline: Digital Payments Penetration Reaches New Highs in Rural India, Driving Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) indicates a significant surge in digital payments adoption in rural and semi-urban areas of India.
• Initiatives like the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), Jan Dhan accounts, and improved internet connectivity have been pivotal in this expansion.
• The increase in digital transactions is driving financial inclusion, enabling easier access to government benefits, credit, and other financial services for previously unbanked populations.
• Challenges remain, including digital literacy, cybersecurity concerns, and ensuring robust infrastructure in remote areas.
• This trend is crucial for India's vision of a cashless economy and sustainable economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana aims for financial inclusion.
MeitY is responsible for policies on IT, electronics, and internet.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of digital payment systems, particularly UPI, in fostering financial inclusion and transforming rural economies in India.
Discuss the challenges associated with increasing digital payments penetration in rural areas, including digital literacy and infrastructure gaps.
Examine the impact of a cashless economy on various socio-economic segments and its implications for governance and transparency.
Suggest policy measures to enhance cybersecurity and build trust in digital payment platforms among vulnerable populations.
articles.imageDescription: A person in a rural setting making a digital payment using a smartphone.
articles.trivia: India recorded over 11.7 billion UPI transactions in December 2023, totaling over ₹18 lakh crore.

articles.headline: Researchers Discover New Species in Western Ghats, Underscoring Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian researchers has announced the discovery of several new species of flora and fauna in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The findings include a new species of frog, a rare orchid, and several insect species previously unknown to science.
• This discovery reinforces the Western Ghats' status as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect these unique ecosystems from habitat loss, climate change, and invasive species.
• The research contributes valuable data for understanding evolutionary patterns and designing effective biodiversity protection strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's biodiversity hotspots.
Biodiversity hotspots are regions with high levels of endemic species.
Conservation efforts are crucial for protecting unique ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research, conservation policy, and understanding ecosystem health.
Examine the challenges in implementing effective biodiversity conservation strategies in a region with high human population density and developmental pressures.
Suggest policy measures and community-based initiatives to protect the unique flora and fauna of the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of a newly discovered frog species in its natural habitat within the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Urges Enhanced Focus on Gender Budgeting for Equitable Development
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary committee report on gender budgeting has called for a more robust and comprehensive approach to integrating gender perspectives into national and state budgets.
• The committee highlighted that while gender budgeting initiatives exist, their implementation often lacks depth and accountability, leading to limited impact on women's empowerment.
• Recommendations include increased allocation for schemes specifically targeting women, gender-disaggregated data collection, and mandatory gender audits of government programs.
• The report stresses that effective gender budgeting is crucial for achieving equitable development outcomes and fulfilling India's commitments to gender equality.
• It also emphasized the need for capacity building among policymakers and implementers to understand and apply a gender lens in financial planning.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Gender budgeting involves analyzing government budgets from a gender perspective.
It aims to assess the impact of government spending on women and men.
SDG 5 focuses on gender equality.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept and significance of gender budgeting in promoting equitable development and women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the challenges in effective implementation of gender budgeting initiatives at both central and state levels.
Examine the recommendations of the parliamentary committee and suggest further measures to strengthen gender budgeting mechanisms.
Evaluate how gender-disaggregated data and gender audits can enhance accountability and transparency in public spending.
articles.imageDescription: A balanced scale with symbols representing men and women, indicating gender equality in financial allocation.
articles.trivia: India introduced gender budgeting in 2005-06, making it one of the pioneering countries in this area.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Crop Insurance Scheme to Boost Climate Resilience for Farmers
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry has announced a revamped crop insurance scheme, focusing on enhancing climate resilience and providing more comprehensive coverage to farmers.
• The new scheme incorporates advanced technologies like satellite imagery, drones, and AI for accurate assessment of crop losses and faster claim settlement.
• It aims to encourage the adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices by offering premium subsidies for farmers who diversify crops or use drought-resistant varieties.
• The scheme also includes provisions for localized risks and post-harvest losses, addressing long-standing demands from the farming community.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to double farmers' income and adapt to the impacts of climate change on agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Crop insurance schemes aim to protect farmers from crop losses.
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is an existing crop insurance scheme.
Technology like drones and AI are being used for crop loss assessment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical features of the new crop insurance scheme and its potential to address the challenges faced by Indian farmers due to climate change.
Discuss the role of technology in improving the efficiency and transparency of crop insurance programs, particularly in claim settlement.
Examine the policy implications of promoting climate-resilient agriculture and its contribution to food security.
Critically evaluate the existing crop insurance models in India and suggest further reforms needed to make them more farmer-centric and effective.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting crops in a field, with a drone flying overhead, symbolizing technology in agriculture.
articles.trivia: India's agriculture sector employs nearly half of the country's workforce.

articles.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint River Commission Discusses Water Sharing and Flood Management
articles.explainer:
• The India-Bangladesh Joint River Commission (JRC) held its latest meeting to discuss crucial issues related to transboundary river management, including water sharing and flood forecasting.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to finding equitable and sustainable solutions for sharing waters of common rivers, particularly the Teesta and Feni rivers.
• Discussions also focused on enhancing cooperation in flood management, river pollution control, and the development of joint projects for river basin management.
• The JRC plays a vital role in fostering bilateral cooperation and addressing complex water-related challenges that impact millions in both countries.
• This dialogue underscores the importance of regional cooperation for resource management and diplomatic relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The JRC facilitates discussions between India and Bangladesh on common rivers.
Teesta and Feni are important transboundary rivers.
Flood management and water sharing are key areas of cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of transboundary river management for India-Bangladesh bilateral relations and regional stability.
Discuss the complex challenges involved in water sharing agreements for common rivers like the Teesta and Feni.
Examine the role of the Joint River Commission in fostering cooperation and resolving disputes over water resources.
Suggest sustainable strategies for integrated river basin management that can benefit both India and Bangladesh while addressing environmental concerns.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the major rivers flowing between India and Bangladesh, highlighting their shared water resources.
articles.trivia: India shares 54 transboundary rivers with Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Report Calls for Greater Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies to Drive Local Governance
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by a parliamentary committee on urban development has strongly advocated for enhanced financial autonomy and capacity building for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India.
• The report highlighted that ULBs often struggle with inadequate financial resources, limiting their ability to provide essential services and undertake development projects.
• Recommendations include increasing the share of state taxes devolved to ULBs, improving their own revenue generation mechanisms, and providing better access to capital markets.
• Greater financial independence is deemed crucial for strengthening local governance, promoting participatory democracy, and achieving sustainable urban development.
• The committee also stressed the need for transparent financial reporting and accountability mechanisms at the local level.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Development processes and the development industry - the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: ULBs are responsible for local governance in urban areas.
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act provided for the establishment of ULBs.
Financial autonomy is crucial for effective local governance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions and practical challenges concerning the financial autonomy of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India.
Discuss the significance of strengthening ULBs for effective local governance and achieving sustainable urban development goals.
Examine the recommendations of the parliamentary committee and suggest innovative strategies for enhancing the revenue generation and financial management of ULBs.
Evaluate the role of state finance commissions and other mechanisms in ensuring adequate devolution of funds to local bodies.
articles.imageDescription: A depiction of a city council meeting, symbolizing local governance and civic administration.
articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Urban Local Bodies, Decentralization of power and finance

articles.headline: Editorial: Strengthening India's Disaster Response Mechanisms Post-Kumbh Tragedy
articles.explainer:
• The recent crowd crush at the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela tragically underscores the perennial challenges India faces in managing large-scale events and disasters.
• This editorial calls for an immediate and thorough review of existing disaster management protocols, particularly for mass gatherings, focusing on prevention rather than just response.
• It advocates for greater investment in smart crowd management technologies, predictive analytics, and robust communication systems.
• The need for enhanced inter-agency coordination, regular drills, and public awareness campaigns is paramount to prevent recurrence.
• The tragedy must serve as a catalyst for systemic reforms in public safety infrastructure and policy across the nation.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key lessons learned from the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela crowd crush for India's disaster management framework.
Discuss the role of technology (e.g., AI, IoT, surveillance) in modernizing crowd management and disaster preparedness for mass gatherings.
Examine the institutional and policy reforms needed to strengthen inter-agency coordination and accountability in public safety operations.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy for integrating disaster risk reduction into urban planning and event management in India.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration symbolizing a hand protecting a crowd, representing effective disaster management.
articles.trivia: India established the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) in 2006 under the Disaster Management Act, 2005.

articles.headline: Opinion: Balancing Development and Environmental Protection – A Critical Imperative for India
articles.explainer:
• This opinion piece argues that India's rapid economic growth often comes at the cost of environmental degradation, leading to a critical need for policy re-evaluation.
• It highlights the inherent conflict between developmental aspirations (e.g., infrastructure projects, industrial expansion) and ecological conservation (e.g., forest protection, pollution control).
• The author advocates for adopting a 'green growth' model, integrating environmental impact assessments more rigorously, and promoting sustainable development practices.
• Emphasizes the importance of public participation, environmental education, and robust regulatory frameworks to achieve this balance.
• The piece concludes that long-term prosperity hinges on prioritizing ecological sustainability alongside economic advancement.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Growth, Development and Employment
mainsPoints: Discuss the inherent conflict between India's developmental imperatives and the need for environmental protection. How can this 'development vs. environment' dilemma be resolved?
Analyze the concept of 'green growth' and its relevance for India's economic trajectory, citing examples of successful implementation.
Examine the role of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) in balancing developmental projects with ecological concerns and suggest reforms to strengthen them.
Evaluate the challenges in fostering public participation and environmental awareness to promote sustainable development practices across sectors.
articles.imageDescription: A split image showing industrial development on one side and a lush green forest on the other, with a balancing scale in the middle.
articles.trivia: India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement, committing to reduce its emissions intensity by 45% by 2030 from 2005 levels.

articles.headline: New Species of Fish Discovered in Ganga Basin, Highlights Riverine Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• Ichthyologists from an Indian university have announced the discovery of a new species of freshwater fish in the middle stretch of the Ganga River basin.
• The discovery underscores the rich, yet underexplored, biodiversity of India's major river systems, which are facing significant ecological pressures.
• The newly identified species belongs to the carp family and exhibits unique morphological characteristics.
• Scientists emphasize the importance of river conservation efforts, including pollution control and habitat restoration, to protect such aquatic life.
• This finding contributes to the taxonomic knowledge of Indian freshwater fauna and highlights the need for continuous research and monitoring.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Ganga River is one of India's most significant river systems.
Riverine ecosystems are rich in biodiversity.
Pollution and habitat degradation threaten aquatic species.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up image of a small, colorful freshwater fish, representing the newly discovered species.
articles.trivia: The Ganges River Dolphin, an endangered species, is India's national aquatic animal.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Discuss Enhanced Maritime Connectivity and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Sri Lanka met to explore avenues for further enhancing maritime connectivity and boosting bilateral economic cooperation.
• Discussions focused on improving port infrastructure, restarting ferry services, and streamlining customs procedures to facilitate trade and tourism.
• Both nations emphasized the strategic importance of their maritime neighborhood and the potential for joint development projects in the Indian Ocean region.
• The talks also touched upon ongoing cooperation in defense and security, including maritime surveillance and information sharing.
• This engagement aims to strengthen the enduring cultural and economic ties between the two South Asian neighbors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka are maritime neighbors.
Increased maritime connectivity facilitates trade and tourism.
India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy emphasizes strong ties with neighbors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka relations, particularly in the context of maritime security and economic cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the potential benefits of enhanced maritime connectivity (e.g., ferry services, port development) for bilateral trade and people-to-people exchanges.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy, with a specific focus on its engagement with island nations like Sri Lanka.
Evaluate the role of joint development projects in fostering long-term stability and prosperity in the India-Sri Lanka relationship.
articles.imageDescription: A ship sailing between the coasts of India and Sri Lanka, symbolizing maritime links.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait separates India from Sri Lanka, and the Sethusamudram project aims to create a shipping canal through it.

highlights: A tragic crowd crush at the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela claims at least 30 lives, prompting a review of disaster management for mass gatherings.
Parliament passes the National Education Technology Board Bill 2025, aiming to integrate AI and ML into India's education system.
RBI maintains the repo rate at 6.5% amidst persistent inflation concerns, signaling a cautious approach to monetary policy.
India and the UK enter the final phase of FTA negotiations, with key discussions on services and intellectual property rights.
ISRO successfully launches 'EOS-08', an advanced Earth Observation Satellite, enhancing India's remote sensing capabilities for national development.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent tragedy at Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, consider the following statements regarding 'Crowd Crush' incidents:
1. They primarily occur due to structural failure of temporary infrastructure.
2. Inadequate crowd management and insufficient entry/exit points are major contributing factors.
3. Technological solutions like AI-powered surveillance can significantly mitigate risks.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While structural failure can contribute, crowd crushes are primarily human-made disasters caused by high crowd density, inadequate flow management, and panic, rather than just structural failure. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. Inadequate crowd management, poor planning of entry/exit points, and lack of clear pathways are major factors. Technology like AI surveillance, real-time density monitoring, and predictive analytics can help manage crowds and prevent crushes.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the policy interest rates in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) that is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India. While RBI implements the policy, the MPC makes the decision.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about the National Education Technology Board (NETB) Bill 2025:
1. It proposes the establishment of a statutory body to integrate technology in education.
2. Its primary focus is on promoting traditional teaching methods over digital tools.
3. Concerns about data privacy and the digital divide have been raised regarding its implementation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the Bill aims to establish NETB as a statutory body. Statement 2 is incorrect; the Bill's focus is on integrating technology, including AI/ML, into education, not promoting traditional methods over digital tools. Statement 3 is correct, as digital divide and data privacy are common concerns with large-scale technology integration in education.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following sectors are currently outside the ambit of Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Petroleum products (crude oil, petrol, diesel, aviation turbine fuel, natural gas) and alcohol for human consumption are currently outside the purview of GST. They are subject to excise duty by the central government and VAT/sales tax by state governments. All other options are already under GST.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to India's 'Neighbourhood First Policy', which of the following statements is most accurate?
options: (a) It prioritizes diplomatic relations with Western countries over immediate neighbors.
(b) It focuses on enhancing economic and strategic ties with immediate neighbors.
(c) It primarily aims to counter China's influence in the Indian Ocean Region.
(d) It seeks to establish India as a dominant military power in South Asia.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It focuses on enhancing economic and strategic ties with immediate neighbors.
prelimsMocks.explanation: India's 'Neighbourhood First Policy' is a foreign policy initiative that emphasizes cordial and cooperative relations with its immediate South Asian neighbors. The core objective is to enhance economic, diplomatic, and strategic ties, promoting regional peace and prosperity. While countering influence and military power might be underlying considerations, the primary stated goal is cooperation and mutual benefit.

mainsMocks.question: India's demographic dividend presents a unique window of opportunity, yet rapid urbanization poses significant challenges. Discuss how strategic investments in education, skill development, and sustainable urban planning can convert this dividend into sustained economic growth and inclusive development. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies. GS3: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce demographic dividend and urbanization trends in India. Explain opportunities (large workforce, innovation). Detail challenges (infrastructure strain, unemployment, slums). Propose solutions: quality education, relevant skill development (vocational training, future skills), job creation in both urban/rural areas. Emphasize sustainable urban planning (smart cities, public transport, green infrastructure). Conclude with the importance of integrated policy for inclusive growth.

mainsMocks.question: The recent crowd crush at the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela highlights the recurring challenges in disaster management for mass gatherings in India. Critically analyze the systemic issues contributing to such tragedies and suggest comprehensive measures, including technological interventions and policy reforms, to enhance public safety and disaster preparedness. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Disaster and disaster management. GS3: Security challenges and their management.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by acknowledging the tragedy and its implications. Identify systemic issues: inadequate crowd control, poor infrastructure planning, weak communication, lack of real-time monitoring, insufficient emergency response, and limited inter-agency coordination. Propose solutions: smart crowd management (AI, IoT, CCTV), predictive analytics, robust early warning systems, standardized protocols, regular drills, capacity building for personnel, public awareness campaigns, and clear accountability mechanisms. Emphasize a shift from reactive to proactive disaster management.

mainsMocks.question: The government's new policy framework for Green Hydrogen production aims to position India as a global hub. Discuss the economic and environmental significance of this initiative, outlining the challenges India must overcome to achieve its ambitious targets in the green hydrogen sector. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Green Hydrogen initiative and its goal. Explain economic significance (energy security, new industries, job creation, export potential) and environmental significance (decarbonization, climate change mitigation, reduced pollution). Highlight challenges: high production costs, need for massive renewable energy infrastructure, water availability for electrolysis, storage and transportation issues, technological gaps, and policy consistency. Conclude with the need for sustained investment, R&D, and international collaboration.

GS1.headline: Tragedy Strikes Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela: At Least 30 Dead, Dozens Injured in Crowd Crush
GS1.explainer:
• A devastating crowd crush occurred at the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, claiming at least 30 lives and injuring over 60.
• The incident reportedly took place near a major bathing ghat during a significant ritual, leading to chaos among pilgrims.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the causes, focusing on crowd management protocols, entry/exit points, and emergency response mechanisms.
• This incident highlights critical challenges in managing large religious gatherings, emphasizing the need for advanced planning, technology deployment, and inter-agency coordination.
• The Prime Minister and Chief Minister have expressed condolences and announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: The 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is being held in Prayagraj (formerly Allahabad).
Kumbh Mela is one of the largest peaceful gatherings in the world, held every 12 years at four different locations.
Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides for effective management of disasters.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic failures in crowd management at large religious gatherings in India and suggest comprehensive reforms.
Discuss the role of technology (e.g., AI, surveillance, data analytics) in preventing and responding to crowd crushes.
Evaluate the responsibilities of state and central governments in ensuring safety at events of national and international significance like the Kumbh Mela.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such tragedies on the local community and the broader religious tourism sector.
GS1.imageDescription: Aerial view of a large gathering at the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, with emergency services responding to an incident.
GS1.trivia: The Kumbh Mela was inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2017.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Vulnerable sections of society, role of NGOs

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Site in Odisha, Reveals Rich Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastery site in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has revealed well-preserved stupas, monastic cells, statues of Buddha, and intricate terracotta panels dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• This finding adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, which is already known for sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri.
• The discovery is expected to provide new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural styles.
• Conservation efforts are underway, and plans for developing the site for tourism and academic research are being considered.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage (e.g., Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri).
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the evolution and spread of Buddhism in India.
Examine the challenges and best practices in conserving ancient archaeological sites while promoting tourism and research.
Analyze the contribution of Odisha's historical sites to India's cultural tapestry and their potential for socio-economic development.
Evaluate the role of ASI in protecting and promoting India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient site, revealing parts of a stupa and ancient pottery.
GS1.trivia: The first Director-General of the Archaeological Survey of India was Alexander Cunningham in 1871.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights India's Demographic Dividend Amidst Rapid Urbanization Challenges
GS1.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog underscores India's unique position to harness its demographic dividend over the next two decades, with a large working-age population.
• However, the report also warns about the escalating challenges posed by rapid and often unplanned urbanization, including strain on infrastructure, housing, and public services.
• It recommends strategic investments in education, skill development, and job creation in both urban and semi-urban areas to fully capitalize on the youth bulge.
• The report also calls for innovative urban planning, smart city initiatives, and sustainable infrastructure development to accommodate population shifts.
• Failure to address these challenges could transform the demographic dividend into a demographic disaster.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Effects of globalization on Indian society
prelimsPoints: Demographic dividend refers to the economic growth potential that can result from shifts in a population’s age structure.
NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
Urbanization presents challenges like infrastructure strain and housing shortages.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges presented by India's demographic dividend in the context of rapid urbanization.
Discuss the policy interventions required in education, skill development, and employment generation to leverage the youth population effectively.
Examine the strategies for sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development to manage the pressures of increasing urban populations.
Critically evaluate the 'demographic disaster' scenario and the preventive measures India must undertake.
GS1.imageDescription: A composite image showing a bustling city skyline alongside young, educated individuals, representing urbanization and demographic dividend.
GS1.trivia: India is projected to become the world's most populous country by 2027, surpassing China.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes National Education Technology Board Bill 2025, Aims to Integrate AI in Learning
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian Parliament has successfully passed the National Education Technology Board (NETB) Bill, 2025, marking a significant step towards modernizing the education sector.
• The Bill proposes the establishment of a statutory body, the NETB, to facilitate the integration of technology, especially Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), into teaching and learning processes.
• Its mandate includes developing national standards for educational technology, recommending digital curricula, and overseeing the deployment of AI-powered learning tools.
• Critics raise concerns about digital divide, data privacy, and the potential impact on traditional teaching methods.
• Proponents highlight the potential for personalized learning, wider access to quality education, and preparing students for future job markets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Education
prelimsPoints: The NETB Bill 2025 aims to integrate technology in education.
The proposed NETB will be a statutory body.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 laid emphasis on the use of technology in education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of integrating AI and ML in India's education system, considering socio-economic disparities.
Discuss how the NETB can ensure equitable access to digital education tools and mitigate the digital divide.
Critically evaluate the provisions of the NETB Bill 2025 regarding data privacy and ethical AI use in educational settings.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in scaling up educational technology initiatives across the country.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image showing students interacting with AI-powered learning platforms.
GS2.trivia: India's digital learning platform, SWAYAM, offers online courses for free to students across various disciplines.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Development processes and the development industry - the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

GS2.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Focus on Services and IP
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their conclusive phase, with both nations pushing to finalize the deal.
• Key sticking points remain in areas such as market access for services, intellectual property rights, and rules of origin.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially doubling trade by 2030.
• India is seeking greater access for its professionals and textiles, while the UK is keen on reducing tariffs on Scotch whisky and automotive products.
• A successful agreement is anticipated to strengthen strategic ties and diversify supply chains for both countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and the UK are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
Services and Intellectual Property are key areas of negotiation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-UK FTA for India's foreign policy and economic objectives.
Discuss the potential impact of the FTA on key Indian sectors such as IT services, textiles, and agriculture.
Examine the challenges in negotiating comprehensive FTAs, particularly concerning sensitive sectors and intellectual property rights.
Evaluate how this FTA could reshape India's trade relations with other major economies post-Brexit.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The UK is one of India's largest trading partners and a significant source of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI).

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Mission Poshan 2.0' to Combat Malnutrition with Integrated Approach
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched 'Mission Poshan 2.0', an integrated nutrition support program aimed at tackling malnutrition across India.
• The mission consolidates existing nutrition-related schemes and introduces new components focusing on dietary diversity, traditional food systems, and community-based management of severe acute malnutrition.
• It emphasizes the use of technology for real-time monitoring, data analytics, and last-mile delivery of services through Anganwadi centers.
• The program seeks to address both undernutrition and micronutrient deficiencies among children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
• 'Mission Poshan 2.0' aligns with India's commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: 'Mission Poshan 2.0' is a nutrition support program.
It targets children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Anganwadi centers play a crucial role in its implementation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of malnutrition in India and how 'Mission Poshan 2.0' attempts to address them through an integrated approach.
Discuss the role of technology, community participation, and inter-sectoral convergence in the effective implementation of nutrition programs.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving nutrition security in India, particularly in remote and vulnerable communities.
Examine how 'Mission Poshan 2.0' aligns with global targets like SDG 2 and India's progress towards these goals.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a balanced diet and healthy children, symbolizing the goals of Mission Poshan.
GS2.trivia: The Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

GS2.headline: Indian Naval Ship Participates in Multinational Maritime Exercise 'MILAN 2025'
GS2.explainer:
• An Indian Naval Ship (INS) has joined several other nations in the multinational maritime exercise 'MILAN 2025' in the Andaman Sea.
• 'MILAN' is a biennial multilateral naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy, aiming to enhance interoperability and foster naval cooperation among friendly navies.
• This year's exercise focuses on anti-submarine warfare, anti-piracy operations, and humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) scenarios.
• The participation underscores India's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening maritime security partnerships.
• The exercise provides a platform for participating navies to share best practices and build confidence in joint operations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: 'MILAN' is a biennial multilateral naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy.
It takes place in the Andaman Sea.
HADR operations are a key focus of such exercises.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of multilateral naval exercises like 'MILAN' for India's maritime security and foreign policy objectives in the Indo-Pacific.
Analyze how such exercises contribute to building interoperability and trust among regional navies, especially in HADR scenarios.
Examine the evolving security landscape in the Indo-Pacific and India's role in maintaining a free, open, and inclusive region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of defense diplomacy and military cooperation in strengthening bilateral and multilateral relations.
GS2.imageDescription: Warships from various nations sailing in formation during a naval exercise.
GS2.trivia: The MILAN exercise was first initiated in 1995 with the participation of four littoral states.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Climate Finance Working Group Meeting, Pushes for Equitable Climate Action
GS2.explainer:
• India is hosting the first G20 Climate Finance Working Group (CFWG) meeting of the year, focusing on mobilizing funds for climate action in developing countries.
• The meeting aims to formulate strategies for increasing climate finance flows, enhancing accessibility, and addressing the debt burden faced by many nations.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate financing.
• Discussions include innovative financing mechanisms, adaptation funding, and technology transfer to support a just energy transition.
• The outcomes of this meeting are crucial for setting the agenda for climate negotiations at upcoming international forums.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: G20 is an international forum for the governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
CBDR-RC is a principle under the UNFCCC.
Climate finance aims to support mitigation and adaptation actions.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's position and priorities at the G20 Climate Finance Working Group meeting, particularly concerning equitable climate action.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing adequate and accessible climate finance for developing countries and the role of developed nations.
Examine the concept of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate innovative financing mechanisms and policy frameworks that can accelerate the just energy transition in developing economies.
GS2.imageDescription: The G20 logo with flags of member countries, set against a backdrop of renewable energy infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: The G20 accounts for about 85% of global GDP, 75% of global trade, and two-thirds of the world population.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Urges Enhanced Focus on Gender Budgeting for Equitable Development
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary committee report on gender budgeting has called for a more robust and comprehensive approach to integrating gender perspectives into national and state budgets.
• The committee highlighted that while gender budgeting initiatives exist, their implementation often lacks depth and accountability, leading to limited impact on women's empowerment.
• Recommendations include increased allocation for schemes specifically targeting women, gender-disaggregated data collection, and mandatory gender audits of government programs.
• The report stresses that effective gender budgeting is crucial for achieving equitable development outcomes and fulfilling India's commitments to gender equality.
• It also emphasized the need for capacity building among policymakers and implementers to understand and apply a gender lens in financial planning.

Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
